summary.intent: Fix a RTL (right-to-left) keyboard navigation bug in the TagGroup component and get it through code review (Codex) and CI on PR #694 in fogodev/ars-ui.
summary.outcome: The RTL fix was pushed as commit 65417d10, a follow-up clippy lint fix was applied and committed, full coverage remained above thresholds (98.5% lines, 90.1% branch), and the corrected code was pushed for CI to rerun.

repo: The repo enforces `field_reassign_with_default` via Clippy with `-D warnings`, meaning test setup using `let mut x = Default::default(); x.field = val;` will fail CI — struct update syntax or direct construction must be used instead.
CI uses an umbrella `Feature Flag Check` job that fails alongside Clippy when Clippy fails.
Full coverage is run via `cargo xtask ci coverage` and enforces a 90% branch threshold; `ars-components` was at 90.1% branch and 98.5% line coverage at time of push.
Codex is integrated as an automated reviewer triggered via `@codex review` PR comment; it signals pickup with a 'eyes' reaction on the PR.
The repo is `fogodev/ars-ui` and the component lives in `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/tag_group/`.

code.finding: TagGroup keyboard helpers resolve RTL from the active locale and swap horizontal ArrowRight/ArrowLeft intents while leaving vertical arrows unchanged. A test for this was added but originally used field reassignment after `Env::default()`, which violates the `field_reassign_with_default` Clippy lint and was fixed to use struct construction syntax.

workflow: Codex review threads can be resolved via the GitHub GraphQL API (`resolveReviewThread` mutation) after addressing the feedback and replying with the fix commit SHA.
Polling Codex review state requires checking both PR reactions (for pickup signal) and unresolved review threads filtered by author login matching 'codex'.
Running `cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` locally before pushing catches CI lint failures early.
The commit co-author convention used is `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>` for Codex-driven or Codex-reviewed changes.

friction: A Clippy lint failure (`field_reassign_with_default`) was not caught before the first push, requiring a second commit/push cycle and a full re-run of `cargo xtask ci coverage` (which is slow).
Polling for Codex review state requires multiple separate API calls (reactions + GraphQL thread query + CI status), with manual sleep waits between polls.
The CI `Feature Flag Check` umbrella job fails as a side effect of Clippy failure, making the failure surface appear broader than it is.
open_items: CI was still re-running on the final pushed commit at session end — outcome of the Clippy-fixed CI run was not yet confirmed.
Codex had not yet completed its review pass on the updated commits by end of session.
